name: New Heights
description_short: Archer Heights Credit Union merged with Credit Union 1 as of April 2026. Members now access banking services through CU1's platform with maintained loan terms.
description_long:
Archer Heights Credit Union (AHCU) completed a merger with Credit Union 1 (CU1) on April 6, 2026, with the transition now at 100% completion. The merger was a full integration, with all AHCU members automatically converted to CU1 membership and their accounts migrated to CU1's systems. This represents a significant consolidation in the credit union sector, combining member bases and operational infrastructure under the CU1 brand.

CU1 offers traditional credit union banking products including consumer loans (mortgages, home equity loans, auto loans), credit cards, checking and savings accounts, digital and mobile banking, direct deposit, bill pay, debit cards with digital controls, and credit monitoring tools. Members maintain their existing loan rates and terms through the duration of their agreements. The merger preserved member benefits while providing access to CU1's broader product suite and digital infrastructure. Auto loan members must verify their insurance meets CU1's requirements as part of the transition.

The primary distinguishing factor is that this is a post-merger entity. CU1 specifically maintained existing loan terms for converted members, meaning rate changes were not imposed during the transition. The Archer Heights branch location at 6554 W. Archer Avenue in Chicago remains operational with updated hours. CU1 provides comprehensive digital banking capabilities including eStatements (with paper statement fees waived through November 2025), digital wallet, card controls, and credit score monitoring. The merger completion tracking shows 100% fulfillment as of the stated date.

The honest assessment is that this company no longer operates as an independent entity. Consumers banking here are banking with Credit Union 1, not Archer Heights Credit Union. The transition required significant account management on members' part—updating automatic payments, verifying payroll deposits, activating new debit cards, and updating routing numbers (CU1's routing number is 271188081). The original AHCU routing number deactivates February 28, 2026, and old checks are no longer accepted. Members with credit cards must use a separate eZCard portal. The merger disrupted some convenience but preserved core loan terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Consumer loans (mortgages, home equity loans, auto loans) with rate/term preservation
Credit cards managed through eZCard portal
Checking and savings accounts with digital access
Bill payment and external money transfers
Debit card activation and digital card controls
pros: Existing loan rates and terms maintained post-merger for mortgages, home equity loans, and auto loans
Comprehensive digital banking platform with bill pay, money transfers, debit card controls, and credit monitoring
Paper statement fees waived through November 2025 for members who enroll in eDocuments
Physical branch location maintained at 6554 W. Archer Avenue, Chicago with expanded Saturday hours (9am-12pm)
Mobile app and digital wallet functionality for account management on customer's schedule
Customer service support via phone at (800) 252-6950 with dedicated merger question submissions
Savings-only and inactive account fees waived through November 2026
cons: Original Archer Heights routing number deactivates February 28, 2026—all checks must be reordered or payments will fail
Automatic payments and recurring bill pay did not transfer during merger; customers must manually recreate all payment arrangements
ACH payments using old AHCU account/routing number only guaranteed for six months post-merger, requiring urgent member action
Credit card management moved to separate eZCard portal requiring new account setup with third-party vendor
Paper statements incur $5/month fee starting December 1, 2025 unless members proactively enroll in eDocuments
best_for: Archer Heights Credit Union members who want continuity with maintained loan terms after the CU1 merger
Chicago-area consumers seeking branch banking with digital-first capabilities and credit union membership benefits
Customers who value preserved auto loan, mortgage, or home equity loan terms during financial institution transitions
Members needing comprehensive digital banking with multiple payment and monitoring tools in one platform
diagnosis: This profile is best for former Archer Heights Credit Union members understanding their new banking relationship with Credit Union 1 post-merger. The critical caveat is that AHCU no longer operates independently—all banking now occurs through CU1's systems, requiring customers to update routing numbers, recreate payment arrangements, and manage separate portals for different products. The merger is complete but requires active member management to avoid payment failures and service disruptions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
